summary: Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant's machine hall and claimed control of logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk. Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were issued for eastern and south‑eastern fronts, while a Russian Shahed‑2 drone struck a residential building in Romania. Zelensky appealed to the US for licenses to produce Patriot missiles and the US is studying Ukraine's drone warfare experience.
key_points: Ukrainian Third Army Corps announced control over logistics routes in Luhansk and Eastern Slobozhanshchyna, reaching 205 km into enemy‑held territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
A Ukrainian drone hit the machine block building of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, causing a through explosion; radiation levels remained normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the eastern and south‑eastern directions, warning of possible air strikes on frontline regions (Source: @kpszsu/63443).
A Russian Shahed‑2 drone struck a multi‑storey residential building in Romania, causing injuries and material damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81582).
President Zelensky requested US licenses for Ukrainian production of Patriot missile components, citing a shortfall in anti‑ballistic missile supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81581).
An American mercenary, identified as Carsten Trevor Grinchurch, was eliminated in the Dobropillia direction while serving with the 12th operational brigade "Azov" (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30563).
The US Secretary of War noted Washington is actively studying Ukraine's battlefield drone usage to improve rapid scaling and adaptation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30560).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces have extended operational reach, striking the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant and seizing logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk. Russian tactical aviation activity warnings indicate heightened air threat on eastern fronts, while a Russian Shahed‑2 drone attacked a residential building in Romania, prompting diplomatic concern. Zelensky’s appeal for Patriot missile production licenses underscores Ukraine’s push for enhanced air defence amid escalating threats.

points: The Third Army Corps reported control over logistics routes in Luhansk and Eastern Slobozhanshchyna, with UAV pilots reaching over 205 km into enemy‑held territory, striking enemy armour and ammunition depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).

Ukrainian drones struck the machine block building of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, delivering a through explosion; Rosatom confirmed radiation levels remain normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
President Zelensky appealed to the US for licenses to produce Patriot missile components, arguing current US anti‑ballistic missile production is insufficient (Source: @UkraineNow/81581).
An American mercenary, Carsten Trevor Grinchurch, was eliminated in the Dobropillia direction while serving with the 12th operational brigade "Azov" (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30563).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV movements across northern and eastern oblasts, including UAVs from Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk and from Kharkiv near Bohodukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/63464, @kpszsu/63455).
Ukrainian officials highlighted US interest in Ukrainian drone tactics, with the US Secretary of War stating Washington is studying Ukraine's battlefield drone usage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30560).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a Nova Poshta depot in Dnipro, causing the building to burn completely; no casualties were reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were issued for the eastern direction, indicating a threat of air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/63443).
A similar warning was posted for the south‑eastern direction, highlighting potential use of aerial strike assets near the front (Source: @kpszsu/63451).
Russian Shahed‑2 drone attacked a multi‑storey residential building in Romania, causing injuries and material damage, with Romanian officials blaming Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81582).
Russia announced a temporary ban on aviation kerosene exports until 30 Nov 2026 to stabilize its domestic fuel market (Source: @tass_agency/378379).
Russian authorities warned of possible retaliatory strikes on Kyiv, describing the conflict as nearing direct exchanges of attacks with the West (Source: @tass_agency/378358).
Russian officials reported a new law allowing the Russian Armed Forces to protect Russian citizens abroad in cases of foreign legal actions (Source: @tass_agency/378376).
Russia resumed Aeroflot flights from Moscow to Dubai starting 1 June after a three‑month suspension (Source: @tass_agency/378355).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported operating near Savintsi and Zlatopil in Kharkiv oblast, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63442).
Ukrainian forces warned of increased Russian tactical aviation activity on the eastern front, raising the risk of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/63443).
Ukrainian drone activity was noted from the north of Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk, indicating cross‑border reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/63464).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Kharkiv's Bohodukhiv moving southward, suggesting pressure on Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/63455).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a logistics depot in Dnipro, causing a fire but no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).
Russian tactical aviation warnings also covered the south‑eastern direction, affecting the eastern front's southern flank (Source: @kpszsu/63451).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted near Sumy heading toward Krivyi Rih, indicating operational reach into contested zones (Source: @kpszsu/63442).
Ukrainian UAVs operated from the north of Chernihiv along the Belarus border, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63456).
UAVs were reported heading from Zhytomyr toward Korosten from the north (Source: @kpszsu/63461).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed in Sumy oblast moving northward near Nizi (Source: @kpszsu/63422).
Ukrainian UAVs were noted in Chernihiv heading toward Chernihiv city from the north (Source: @kpszsu/63422).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported on the north‑west of Chernihiv along the Belarus border, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63456).
Ukrainian UAV activity was recorded in Poltava near Shyshaky and in Chernihiv near Dobranka (Source: @eRadarrua/94554, @eRadarrua/94564).
Ukrainian UAVs were seen from the north of Sumy heading toward Novaya Sparta (Source: @kpszsu/63467).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the southwest (Source: @kpszsu/63460).
Ukrainian UAVs operated in Dnipropetrovsk region moving north of Tokmakivka toward Solone (Source: @eRadarrua/94554).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed in Kharkiv heading toward Barvinkove (Source: @eRadarrua/94566).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Zaporizhzhia heading toward Vilnyansk (Source: @eRadarrua/94566).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a Nova Poshta depot in Dnipro, causing a total building loss (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Russian tactical aviation warnings for the south‑eastern direction suggest heightened air threat to the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/63451).
Ukrainian UAVs were noted near the Black Sea, forming a quantitative strike group (Source: @eRadarrua/94529).
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were posted for eastern and south‑eastern directions, indicating potential air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/63443, @kpszsu/63451).
Ukrainian drones struck the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant's machine hall, demonstrating capability to target critical infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
A Russian Shahed‑2 drone attacked a residential building in Romania, causing injuries and material damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81582).

Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple cross‑border and frontline missions across northern, eastern, and southern oblasts, as detailed in numerous kpszsu posts (Sources: @kpszsu/63442, @kpszsu/63464, @kpszsu/63455, @kpszsu/63461).
US officials are studying Ukraine's drone warfare tactics to improve rapid scaling and adaptation of drone forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30560).
Russian air‑fuel export ban aims to secure domestic aviation fuel supplies, potentially affecting Russian air operations (Source: @tass_agency/378379).
Russia announced the resumption of Aeroflot flights to Dubai, indicating a partial normalization of civil air activity (Source: @tass_agency/378355).

key_developments: Ukrainian Third Army Corps seized control of logistics routes in Luhansk and Eastern Slobozhanshchyna, reaching 205 km into enemy‑held territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
A Ukrainian drone struck the machine block building of the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant, causing a through explosion while radiation remained normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Russian tactical aviation activity warnings were issued for the eastern front, indicating a heightened threat of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/63443).
A Russian Shahed‑2 drone hit a multi‑storey residential building in Romania, causing injuries and material damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81582).
President Zelensky requested US licenses for Ukrainian production of Patriot missile components, citing a shortfall in anti‑ballistic missile supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81581).
American mercenary Carsten Trevor Grinchurch was eliminated in the Dobropillia direction while serving with the 12th operational brigade "Azov" (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30563).
The US Secretary of War stated Washington is actively studying Ukraine's battlefield drone usage to improve rapid scaling and adaptation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30560).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air activity warnings and the deployment of Shahed drones indicate an escalating aerial threat across multiple fronts, including cross‑border strikes into Romania. Ukrainian UAV operations demonstrate growing reach, targeting critical infrastructure such as the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant and disrupting enemy logistics deep in occupied territories. The presence of foreign mercenaries and Ukraine's push for indigenous Patriot production reflect a widening scope of external involvement and a drive to bolster air defence capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Romanian authorities condemned the Shahed‑2 drone attack and warned of possible diplomatic repercussions against Russia. The United States is reviewing Ukraine's drone tactics and considering policy adjustments, while Zelensky's appeal for Patriot production licenses signals a push for greater Western support. Russian domestic measures, such as the aviation fuel export ban, aim to sustain internal air operations amid increased external pressure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian UAV activity across northern, eastern, and southern oblasts suggests a coordinated effort to gather intelligence and strike logistical nodes. The deep penetration of UAVs into occupied Luhansk highlights improved operational depth and command‑and‑control capabilities. Monitoring of Russian tactical aviation alerts is essential to anticipate potential air strikes, and the cross‑border drone incident in Romania may indicate a shift toward targeting neighboring NATO states.
